pattern: A single linear scan comparing each value against one fixed threshold is all a 'how many meet or exceed a target' question ever needs.
hints: Every employee is judged independently against the same fixed number, so there is no need to look at the array as a whole beyond a simple pass.
For each employee, compare their hours to the target: meeting or exceeding it counts, anything less does not.
Keep a running count and add one every time an employee's hours are greater than or equal to target; return that count once the scan finishes.

edge_inputs.description: nobody comes close to the target
single employee with zero hours and zero target
maximum target against employees with zero hours
a target of zero means every non-negative hour count automatically qualifies
one employee sits exactly one hour below the target
hours and target both at the maximum value

stress.description: random small workforce against a random target
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 50))
    hours = [rng.randint(0, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    target = rng.randint(0, 100000)
    return [hours, target]
